summary: The team discussed building a restaurant app that allows customers to vote on menu items to optimize offerings. They explored voting methods, authentication, and the need for a simple user experience. A dual-interface system (customer vs staff) was proposed, with analytics to guide decisions. Data tracking, scalability, and inventory integration were highlighted as important. Sarah will consolidate requirements for the next session.

details: Customers vote or rate menu items
Menu evolves based on popularity
Goal is to optimize menu and reduce waste
Voting must be quick and simple (1–2 taps)
Preference for visual rating systems (stars or reactions)
Mobile-friendly and fast interface
Customer app sections: Home/About, Menu, Contact, Location
Debate between anonymous vs authenticated voting
Anonymous voting risks spam and unreliable data
Lightweight login (Google/Apple) suggested as a balance
Track ratings over time
Identify trends and seasonal preferences
Analytics to include top-rated and low-performing dishes
Potential alerts for underperforming dishes
Strong data tracking required from the beginning
Link dish popularity to ingredient usage
Support inventory optimization and stock level suggestions
Requires backend tracking of ingredients per dish
Dual interface: customer-facing app and internal staff dashboard
Customers see menu and ratings only
Staff see ingredients, inventory, and analytics
Backend should be scalable for future expansion
Encourage voting through subtle prompts
Optional notifications considered for future phase

decisions: Use a simple and quick voting system
Adopt a dual-interface system (customer and staff)
Restrict ingredient visibility to staff only
Focus on analytics to support decision-making

action_items.task: Consolidate discussion into initial product requirements
Define backend requirements for ingredient and inventory tracking
Outline analytics metrics and tracking strategy
Design initial UX concepts for voting and navigation

open_questions.question: Should voting be anonymous or require authentication in the final version?
What specific voting mechanism will be implemented (stars, reactions, thumbs up/down)?
How exactly will ingredient tracking be structured in the backend?
What strategies will be most effective to encourage user participation in voting?
